# About {.unnumbered}

The Statistical Grimoire is a beginner-friendly open-access textbook actively being updated with statistical and data analysis techniques using the R programming language and other open-source tools.

This book was created to give students a solid, practical, and free introduction to both statistics and R programming, especially if they have not done any coding before. 

::: callout-caution
You're reading a what amounts to a friendly beta version (it literally only has one complete chapter so far). Check back for additions and improvements.
:::

<hr>

The Statistical Grimoire: Modern Statistical Approaches for the Natural Sciences Using R by Jeffrey M. Pisklak is licensed under <a href = "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/">Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International</a>.

<a href = "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/"><img src = "imgs/preface/by-nc-nd.svg"></a>

<a href = "https://github.com/statistical-grimoire/book"><img src = "imgs/preface/GitHub_Invertocat_Black_Clearspace.svg" style="height:1.5em; width:auto;" target = "_blank">https://github.com/statistical-grimoire/book</a>

<hr>

This is a [Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/books) book. To learn more about Quarto books visit [https://quarto.org/docs/books](https://quarto.org/docs/books).


This book uses the Braille Institute's Hyperlegible family of fonts. To learn more visit: [https://www.brailleinstitute.org/freefont/](https://www.brailleinstitute.org/freefont/)

- **Body Typeface:** Atkinson Hyperlegible Next
- **Code Typeface:** `Atkinson Hyperlegible Mono Font`
